content:
I get the following error when building x86_64 defconfig:

error[E0602]: unknown lint: `l,__fatal_warnings`
  |
  = note: requested on the command line with `-W l,__fatal_warnings`
  = note: `-D unknown-lints` implied by `-D warnings`
  = help: to override `-D warnings` add `#[allow(unknown_lints)]`

Did anyone see that before? It is when running HOSTRUSTC:

HOSTRUSTC scripts/generate_rust_target
Which tag/branch etc.?
v6.18. It is probably something in my setup?
It only happens with defconfig though. Not my usual smaller config.

I just tried locally with v6.18 just in case, LLVM=1 defconfig rust.config and I don't see it.

There is no __fatal_warnings in the repository either. Do you have some custom env vars or similar?
I'll dig some more. I don't usually build defconfig, so maybe I just broke something in my setup.

It is this one:

# HOSTRUSTC scripts/generate_rust_target
  rustc --out-dir scripts/ --emit=dep-info=scripts/.generate_rust_target.d -Clinker-flavor=gcc -Clinker=/nix/store/0z4q9scnxa3pbnw86pigxp6l7j43bawm-clang-wrapper-18.1.8/bin/clang -Clink-args='  -Wl,--fatal-warnings' --edition=2021 -Zbinary_dep_depinfo=y -Astable_features -Dnon_ascii_idents -Dunsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn -Wmissing_docs -Wrust_2018_idioms -Wunreachable_pub -Wclippy::all -Wclippy::as_ptr_cast_mut -Wclippy::as_underscore -Wclippy::cast_lossless -Wclippy::ignored_unit_patterns -Wclippy::mut_mut -Wclippy::needless_bitwise_bool -Aclippy::needless_lifetimes -Wclippy::no_mangle_with_rust_abi -Wclippy::ptr_as_ptr -Wclippy::ptr_cast_constness -Wclippy::ref_as_ptr -Wclippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks -Wclippy::unnecessary_safety_comment -Wclippy::unnecessary_safety_doc -Wrustdoc::missing_crate_level_docs -Wrustdoc::unescaped_backticks -O -Cstrip=debuginfo -Zallow-features=  -Dwarnings   --emit=link=scripts/generate_rust_target /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/xarray-entry/scripts/generate_rust_target.rs
error[E0602]: unknown lint: `l,__fatal_warnings`
  |
  = note: requested on the command line with `-W l,__fatal_warnings`
  = note: `-D unknown-lints` implied by `-D warnings`
  = help: to override `-D warnings` add `#[allow(unknown_lints)]`

error: aborting due to 1 previous error

It is probably nixos related.
This looks like shell escaping issue
The flag is parsed by rustc instead of carried to clang

This fixes my issue:

diff --git a/scripts/Makefile.host b/scripts/Makefile.host
index c1dedf646a39f..4ba0ad6f5a412 100644
--- a/scripts/Makefile.host
+++ b/scripts/Makefile.host
@@ -92,7 +92,7 @@ hostcxx_flags  = -Wp,-MMD,$(depfile) \
 # modules case.
 hostrust_flags = --out-dir $(dir $@) --emit=dep-info=$(depfile) \
         -Clinker-flavor=gcc -Clinker=$(HOSTCC) \
-        -Clink-args='$(call escsq,$(KBUILD_HOSTLDFLAGS))' \
+        -Clink-args='$(strip $(call escsq,$(KBUILD_HOSTLDFLAGS)))' \
                  $(KBUILD_HOSTRUSTFLAGS) $(HOST_EXTRARUSTFLAGS) \
                  $(HOSTRUSTFLAGS_$(target-stem))
I don't know why, but the extra space in front of -Wl is confusing the tools. Probably the nixos compiler wrapper script.
My setup also has the extra space at front. Tried with oxalica's rust-overlay and cannot reproduce the issue
What are you passing as linker to this command?
That is, what is your HOSTCC?
I don't think it is rustc misunderstanding, I think, it is the wrapped clang.
-Clinker=clang -Clink-args=' -Wl,--fatal-warnings'
and my clang is a wrapped clang
So clang from PATH? What does it resolve to?
/nix/store/jf9ad61205k4700ckrwf3wqxfynkdfap-clang-wrapper-18.1.8/bin/clang
Ok, very similar.
Let me rebuild my direnv. I think I might have not rebuilt it since NixOS 25.11
I got clang from nixos-25.11.
I think the env I was using was still using NixOS-25.05. Rebuilding env now
which LLVM version are you using?
cannot reproduce
I'll dig some more after LPC
Thanks for testing.
I made my devShell available here in case you want to compare against yours: https://github.com/nbdd0121/nix-collection/blob/trunk/dev/linux.nix
Whatever this was, I cannot reproduce it any longer :shrug:
Andreas Hindborg has marked this topic as resolved.
